signature_moments: THE COLD OPEN — the alarm doesn't start with a tone; it starts with 5 seconds of your rival saying your name and your streak day ('Oi, Maya. Day 24. He's already awake.') before the alarm swell. Nobody else wakes you up inside a story. It's also the most quoted/clipped moment for TikTok ('my alarm just called me out by name').

THE WAKE CARD MINT — a daily, timestamped, anime-poster proof artifact with your real wake time and real sky photo, minted with a single satisfying flip+shing. BeReal authenticity × gacha-pull dopamine, exported clean for stories. Missed days become 'unaired episodes' — visible gaps that motivate without red-badge shame.

THE NARRATOR REMEMBERS — episodes are generated against your actual log: use the 90-second grace and tomorrow's cold open opens with 'You bought ninety seconds yesterday. Was it worth it?' Wake before your alarm and the rival is genuinely rattled. Continuity is the moat — day 30 audio cannot feel canned because it's literally about you.

TO BE CONTINUED — closing the app after confirming tomorrow's alarm shows a 1.5s black title card with the gold 'TO BE CONTINUED →' stinger and tomorrow's episode number. It reframes going to bed as a cliffhanger, costs zero taps, and is the only app whose best moment happens when you leave it.

ARC FINALES — every ~2 weeks an arc completes: a one-time 2-minute finale episode recaps your real stats (avg wake time, fastest quest, the day you almost broke) in full dramatic narration, and mints a special holographic Arc Card. Season structure gives day-30 users a reason the story keeps escalating instead of repeating.
concepts.why_user_loves_it: At day 30 the app has never once gotten in their way — the alarm has fired flawlessly, every morning screen had exactly one thing to do, and they've never hunted through a menu half-asleep. That reliability is the floor; the love comes from accumulation: a wall of 30 cards that is *their* story (real timestamps, real skies, real titles), an Arc 2 finale that narrated their actual stats back at them, and a rival who has 30 days of receipts and keeps escalating — the content gets MORE personal over time, the opposite of every motivation app that gets stale. The streak isn't a guilt chain; it's a season they're starring in, and missed days are just unaired episodes the narrator writes around. They stay because three feelings renew daily: 'it actually got me up' (utility trust), 'that line was about ME' (protagonist feeling), and 'I need to post this card' (social proof) — and because deleting the app now means cancelling their own show mid-season."

concepts.name: WakeSaga — "Cold Open"
concepts.tagline: Every morning is Episode One. Get up like the show depends on it.
concepts.first_5_seconds: First launch is one screen, no carousel: a near-black "4AM blue" background, a giant SF Pro Display Black time picker glowing ember-orange, and a single line above it — "Your saga starts tomorrow at". The user types their name on the next screen and instantly watches a manga title card stamp itself onto the screen with a heavy haptic thud: "EPISODE 001 — The Morning [Name] Stopped Snoozing." In 5 seconds they understand the whole product: this is an alarm clock, my morning is an anime episode, and I am the protagonist. Every subsequent screen obeys the same rule — one giant element, one verb, readable by a half-asleep brain at arm's length: the alarm screen says only the quest; the quest screen is the camera/sensor itself; the player has one button. If a screen needs explaining, it gets cut.
concepts.core_loop:
NIGHT (the cold open is set): At a user-chosen wind-down time, a gentle notification: "Episode 048 airs at 6:30. Tonight's preview is ready." Opening it shows the Tonight screen — tomorrow's episode number, the quest type drawn for tomorrow (sealed, shown as a face-down card), and a 15-second audio teaser in the narrator's voice ("Rest. Tomorrow we take the cold."). Confirming the alarm starts a lock-screen Live Activity: a tiny dimming ember in the Dynamic Island and "T-7h 42m to Ep.048" on the Lock Screen. In StandBy mode the phone becomes a night clock with the ember slowly pulsing — the phone on the nightstand is already in-universe.

ALARM (Dawn Takeover): At 6:30 AlarmKit fires through silence and Focus. Full-screen takeover: black, then the user's personalized alarm sting (8-12s urgent AI audio, pre-generated: "Forty-seven days. Don't make this the one that breaks."). The screen shows ONE thing in 96pt type: the quest. "STAND UP. PHONE ABOVE YOUR HEAD." There is no dismiss button anywhere. A small "snooze" exists by law of mercy, bottom corner, 28pt, labeled "Rival gains 9 min" — tapping it plays one cutting rival voice line and the ember dims. The audio loops with rising intensity until motion sensors confirm the quest start.

WAKE QUEST (30-90 seconds, body before brain): The phone IS the interface — accelerometer confirms standing, camera viewfinder opens for sky photo / made bed / water glass, pedometer counts 20 steps, pushup counter uses proximity sensor. Progress is a single filling ink-stroke ring. No typing, no reading paragraphs, no decisions. Completion triggers the TITLE CARD DROP: speed-lines flash, a manga panel smashes in with today's episode title + the user's name + a CoreHaptics "power-up" pattern (three rising taps, one heavy thud). This is the daily screenshot moment.

REWARD (Morning Episode): The 60-120s Gemini-TTS Morning Episode auto-plays — the narrator recaps yesterday, names today's mission and obstacle (pulled from the user's stated goals + calendar context), and ends on a hook. While it plays, the title card slowly parallaxes and dialogue renders line-by-line like subtitles. At the end, the WAKE CARD mints: today's collectible — the actual sky/bed photo halftoned into a manga panel, stamped with wake time, episode number, weather, and a rarity foil if conditions were earned (woke before sunrise = "Daybreaker" foil). One tap shares it as a 9:16 story.

DAY (Lock In): Before gym/study/work, the user triggers a 20-30s Lock In clip — from the app's center tab, the Home Screen widget, the Action Button, or the Dynamic Island. Pick a context chip, the narrator delivers a hit of protagonist energy, the Island shows a small "LOCKED IN" timer if they start a focus block.

EVENING (End Credits): A 30-second wrap: the narrator closes today's episode over a slow credits scroll of what the user did (wake time, quests, lock-ins). One question — "Tomorrow's mission?" — answered with one chip tap or 5 words of dictation. Alarm confirmed, Live Activity resumes, loop closes. Total demanded attention per day: under 4 minutes outside the alarm itself.
concepts.information_architecture:
Two-mode architecture, because the user has two brains: a 6:30am lizard brain and an awake browsing brain.

MODE 1 — DAWN RAIL (no navigation): Alarm → Quest → Title Card → Episode → Wake Card is a forced linear modal flow launched by AlarmKit, with zero nav chrome, no back button, no tab bar. You cannot get lost because there is nowhere to go. This is the screen 80% of usage happens on.

MODE 2 — THE APP (3 tabs, awake brain only): a standard iOS tab bar with a raised center action — left "Today", center "Lock In" (prominent circular button, also mapped to Action Button), right "Saga". Settings and narrator management live behind the avatar in Today's top-right corner; they are deliberately buried because config is a once-a-month activity. The paywall ("Casting Call") is a sheet, never a tab. No feed, no social graph, no inbox — sharing happens outward to TikTok/IG via the Wake Card, not inward.

Why this shape: prior structured attempts die because they make the alarm app feel like a dashboard. Here the hierarchy mirrors the day — Today is the present (next episode + this morning's card), Lock In is the verb, Saga is the past (the collection that proves the arc). Three nouns a 16-year-old can hold. Widgets and Live Activities carry the rest of the surface area so the app itself stays small: lock-screen countdown widget, StandBy ember clock, interactive Home Screen widget showing today's Wake Card, Dynamic Island states for pre-alarm ember / episode playback / lock-in timer.

key_screens.job: Make tomorrow feel inevitable: confirm the alarm in one glance and build anticipation for tomorrow's episode.
key_screens.layout: Full-bleed 4AM-blue gradient darkening toward the top like dusk. Top third: 'EPISODE 048' in 17pt mono caps tracking +8%, then the alarm time in 88pt SF Pro Display Black with the ember-orange glow — the time IS the hero, tappable to edit inline. Middle: tomorrow's quest as a face-down card (60% width, manga-panel border, slow 3D idle wobble) — sealed until dawn, tap plays the 15s narrator teaser with a waveform shimmer. Bottom: a single full-width pill CTA 'Lights out' that arms the alarm, starts the Live Activity, and dims the screen to the StandBy ember. Avatar top-right for settings. Nothing else.

key_screens.job: Get the user vertical. Nothing else is allowed to matter.
key_screens.layout: Pure black, launched by AlarmKit over the lock screen. Center: the quest instruction in 96pt Black, max 5 words ('STAND UP. ARM RAISED.'), white with a subtle ember rim-light, pulsing at the audio's tempo. Below it a thin ink-stroke ring that begins filling the moment sensors detect compliance — the ring is the only progress UI. Personalized alarm sting loops with rising layers. Bottom-left corner, deliberately small (28pt, 40% opacity): 'Rival gains 9 min' snooze. No dismiss, no X, no nav. The screen brightness ramps from 20% to 100% over the first 20 seconds — light as a wake mechanic.

key_screens.job: Verify one real-world action using the phone's body, not the user's reading comprehension.
key_screens.layout: The sensor IS the layout. Sky photo: full-screen viewfinder with a halftone overlay frame showing exactly where the sky goes, shutter is the entire bottom 25% of the screen (fat-finger-proof). Steps: a giant live counter '7 / 20' at 120pt center, ring filling around it. Pushups: phone-on-floor mode, screen shows only the count and flashes on each rep with a tick haptic. Persistent top strip: episode number + quest name in 15pt mono. On completion, the screen smash-cuts to the Title Card Drop — speed-lines radiate from center, panel slams in with a heavyImpact haptic, holds 2.5s, auto-saves a screenshot-ready frame.

key_screens.job: Deliver the reward: 60-120 seconds of personalized protagonist audio that frames today's mission.
key_screens.layout: Today's title card art fills the top 55% with slow parallax drift and animated screentone grain. Beneath it, narrator dialogue renders line-by-line as subtitles in 22pt Medium, current line full white, past lines 35% opacity — readable, skimmable, never a wall of text. One control: a 64pt play/pause circle bottom-center; scrub is a thin ink line above it. Dynamic Island shows narrator portrait + waveform so the episode survives backgrounding (they will open TikTok — plan for it). When audio ends, the player collapses upward and the Wake Card mints from the bottom with a card-snap haptic.

key_screens.job: Hand the user today's proof — a collectible they actively want to screenshot and share.
key_screens.layout: Single card centered at 70% screen height: the user's actual quest photo halftone-processed into a manga panel, 2pt ink border, episode number stamped top-left like a chapter mark, wake time + weather + streak count in a receipt-style mono footer. Rarity foils (Daybreaker, First Try, Storm Riser) render as a holographic sheen driven by device gyro — tilt the phone, the foil moves. Two CTAs: full-width primary 'Share to story' (exports clean 9:16, no watermark clutter, just a small WakeSaga chapter stamp), text-button secondary 'Add to Saga'. Swipe down dismisses to Today.

key_screens.job: Show the arc: turn day-30 retention into a shelf the user is proud of and refuses to abandon.
key_screens.layout: Top: current arc as a horizontal chapter strip — 'ARC 5: THE 6AM PROTOCOL', episodes as 10 manga-panel slots filling left to right, finale slot gilded. Arcs replace streak anxiety: a missed day greys a panel as a 'filler episode' with a one-line narrator note ('Even heroes rest. Ep.049 airs tomorrow.') — the saga continues, the streak never 'dies'. Below: the full card binder, a 3-column grid of every Wake Card, foils shimmering on scroll, tap for full-screen with stats. Pinned at top of binder: 'Season Recap' — an auto-generated Sunday audio episode recapping the week. No leaderboards, no friends list; the rival is narrative, not social.

key_screens.job: Deliver a 20-second hit of protagonist energy in under two taps, anywhere.
key_screens.layout: Opens as a half-height sheet even over other contexts (and as the full center tab in-app). Four oversized context chips in a 2x2 grid — GYM, STUDY, WORK, REBUILD — each with its own ink-brush glyph. Tapping one immediately plays the clip: sheet expands, narrator portrait + live waveform, subtitle line, done. Optional 'Start a Lock In block' button afterward spawns a Dynamic Island timer ('LOCKED IN — 47:12') and a Live Activity. The whole interaction is designed for the gym floor: one hand, 8 seconds, gloves on.

key_screens.job: Sell WakeSaga+ as casting your narrator, not unlocking features.
key_screens.layout: Sheet titled 'Who narrates your saga?' Horizontally paged narrator cards (mentor, rival, deadpan senpai, hype captain), each with portrait art, a one-line personality, and a tappable 6-second voice sample — the samples ARE the pitch. Below the carousel, a compact comparison: Free keeps the alarm + one quest + text episodes ('You can always wake up'); WakeSaga+ adds AI voices, generated daily episodes, all quests, custom arcs, and foil cards. Two price pills (annual pre-selected with savings badge, monthly), full-width CTA 'Begin the real arc', restore + terms in footer. Triggered after Episode 3's title card — sell after they've felt the magic, never before.

concepts.design_language:
COLOR: Dark-first, because the app lives at 10pm and 6:30am. Base is "4AM Blue" ink-navy (#0A0E1C), surface cards #131A2E with iOS continuous corners. Single hero accent: "First Light" gradient, ember orange #FF6B35 → magenta #E8336D, used ONLY for the alarm time, progress rings, foils, and primary CTAs — never decoration. Text: off-white #F4F1EA (warm, paper-like, easier at 6am than pure white), secondary at 55% opacity. One semantic green (#3DDC84) reserved exclusively for quest verification. Daytime surfaces (Saga, Lock In) lift to a slightly warmer #11162A so the app reads 'morning' vs 'night' without a theme switch.

TYPE: SF Pro Display for everything interactive — Black (900) for times/counters at 88-120pt, Semibold for CTAs, Regular for body, SF Mono for receipt metadata (timestamps, episode numbers, weather) in 13-15pt caps with +6% tracking. Episode title cards are the ONE place a display voice appears: pre-rendered lettering assets in a brush-stroke style (commissioned, shipped as images, never a live font) so titles feel like anime production art while UI stays native. Dynamic Type supported everywhere except the Dawn Rail, which is already maximum-size by design.

SHAPE & DEPTH: Native iOS geometry — continuous-corner 20pt radius cards, sheets with grabbers, no custom nav bars. The manga language lives in a strict 'panel' system: anything collectible or narrative (Wake Cards, title cards, quest card) gets a 2pt ink border, halftone screentone texture at 8% opacity, and a hard 4pt offset shadow like printed paper. Chrome never gets panels; panels never get blur. Depth comes from gyro parallax on cards and foils, not from drop-shadow soup.

MOTION: 120Hz spring physics throughout (response 0.35, damping 0.8 for sheets; snappier 0.25/0.85 for chips). Two signature transitions: the SMASH CUT (quest complete → title card: 6 frames of radial speed-lines, panel scales from 1.15 to 1.0 with overshoot, paired with heavy haptic) and the CARD MINT (Wake Card slides up with a single confident snap, no confetti ever). Idle states breathe: the ember pulses at 0.5Hz, foils shimmer with gyro. Reduce Motion swaps smash cuts for opacity dissolves.

HAPTICS: A composed CoreHaptics score, not default taps — 'Ember' (soft 0.5Hz heartbeat during pre-alarm Live Activity interactions), 'Power-Up' (three rising transients + one heavy thud on quest complete), 'Mint' (single crisp card-snap), 'Rival' (one low rumble on snooze — consequence you feel). Flutter implementation via a thin platform channel to CHHapticEngine with AHAP files.

ILLUSTRATION: Limited-animation discipline — character art (narrator portraits, title cards, card art) appears only at narrative moments and is high-craft 2D with screentone shading; the UI chrome contains zero mascots, zero stickers, zero anime clip-art. The restraint is the premium: it should feel like Apple shipped an alarm app and a great anime studio did the inserts.
signature_moments: The Title Card Drop — completing the wake quest smash-cuts to a manga title card with today's episode number, a generated episode title, and YOUR name, paired with the Power-Up haptic; it auto-frames as a clean 9:16 screenshot. No alarm app has a daily moment users post at 6:31am.

The Wake Card mint — the day's real sky/bed photo halftoned into a collectible manga panel with receipt-style metadata and gyro-driven holographic foils for earned conditions (pre-sunrise, first-try, bad-weather wake). It is BeReal's authenticity fused with trading-card dopamine, generated from proof of actually getting up.

The ember on the lock screen — from 'Lights out' to dawn, a tiny ember lives in the Dynamic Island and StandBy clock, dimming through the night and flaring 10 minutes before the alarm. The phone on the nightstand is in-universe before the app is even open.

Snooze with a face — snoozing is allowed but personified: 'Rival gains 9 min', one cutting rival voice line, one low rumble haptic. Consequence as story, not as a guilt-trip notification. Competitors either block snooze (hostile) or permit it silently (toothless).

Filler episodes instead of streak death — a missed day greys a panel in the arc as a 'filler episode' with a wry narrator acknowledgment, and the saga continues. Day-30 users stay because the system absorbs failure narratively instead of resetting them to zero, which is the moment every streak app loses this demographic.
concepts.why_user_loves_it: At day 30 the user has a binder of 30 Wake Cards — real photos of 30 real mornings, foils marking the hard ones — and is two episodes from an arc finale, which is a cliffhanger, not a chore. The reward stays variable (tomorrow's quest is sealed nightly, card rarities depend on real conditions, episode scripts evolve with their stated goals), so mornings never flatten into the same notification. Crucially, the app has never once nagged them: the rival taunts in-story, missed days became filler episodes instead of broken streaks, and total daily ask outside the alarm is under four minutes. The narrator now references their actual month ('Remember day 12, the storm?'), which no human in their life does — the parasocial mentor is earned, not scripted. And it keeps producing social currency: title cards and foil Wake Cards are screenshot-native, so posting 'Ep.030' is a flex that markets the app for them. They keep it because it is the only app on their phone where the streak is a story they are inside of, and deleting it would feel like cancelling their own show mid-season.

concepts.name: WakeSaga: Cold Open
concepts.tagline: Every morning is an episode. The alarm is the cold open. Don't let the rival take the lead.
concepts.core_loop:
NIGHT (End Credits, ~60s, ~10:30pm): User opens to a dark, warm "End Credits" screen. Today's stats scroll up like ED credits (wake time, quest completed, lock-ins). Three taps: rate today's episode (5 big stars, MAL-style), type or pick tomorrow's one-line mission ("Leg day before work", "Finish chapter 4"), confirm alarm time on a big wheel. Final tap = "ROLL CREDITS": the day's manga page gets stamped with a halftone freeze-frame "TO BE CONTINUED →", and a 10-second "Next Episode Preview" teaser plays in the narrator's voice ("Jikai — Episode 48: The 6AM Gauntlet"). Overnight, the TTS pipeline generates tomorrow's cold-open line and Morning Episode using the mission, streak state, and rival storyline.

ALARM (Cold Open, 6:30am): Phone fires with an 8–15s in-medias-res audio line — not a beep first, a SCENE: rival voice: "Oi… he's still asleep? After everything he said last night?" Screen is pure black with a massive "EP 48" and ONE giant vermillion button: ANSWER THE CALL. There is no dismiss. A faint gray link offers "filler (snooze 5m) — Rival gains +1." Tapping the button drops the user straight into the Wake Quest: a full-screen, zero-reading physical verification (sky photo, stand-up motion check, water-bottle object hunt, 5 pushups via motion, made-bed photo). The proof IS the interface — viewfinder or motion meter fills the screen.

POST-QUEST (the payoff, ~2 min): One-frame white impact flash + heavy haptic. A black bar slams in diagonally: "EPISODE 48: THE 6AM GAUNTLET" over a daily-generated sunrise background — the screenshot moment. The Morning Episode audio (60–120s, personalized) plays with lower-third anime-style subtitles, framing today's mission, the obstacle, and the FIRST ACTION ("Shoes on before coffee."). Simultaneously the quest proof is minted into a screentoned manga panel — the Wake Panel — with episode number and timestamp. Button: BEGIN EPISODE.

DAY: The Today screen is literally a manga page in progress: panel 1 = title card + mission, panel 2 = this morning's Wake Panel, panel 3 = empty slot labeled TO BE CONTINUED that fills tonight. A LOCK IN pill floats at the bottom: tap → choose GYM / STUDY / WORK / RESET → instant 20–30s fullscreen hype clip with speedlines; each Lock In stamps an "eyecatch" mark on today's page. A thin VS bar tracks your episode count against the rival's.

EVENING: Push at the user's set wind-down time framed as "Today's episode needs an ending." Back to End Credits. If the user snoozed past their quest window in the morning, today is canonically a FILLER EPISODE — page renders gray-toned, narrator is gently disappointed, streak survives but the rival pulls ahead. Canon resumes tomorrow. Every 7–14 days the current arc "binds" into a Volume with a generated cover and goes on the shelf.
concepts.information_architecture: A linear "broadcast" model, not a dashboard. The alarm flow (Cold Open → Wake Quest → Title Card/Morning Episode) is a full-screen modal takeover with no navigation — half-asleep brains get exactly one verb per screen, and anime pacing demands scenes, not menus. The navigable app has exactly THREE bottom-nav surfaces: (1) TODAY — the episode-in-progress manga page and Lock In; this is home. (2) SAGA — the archive: a horizontal shelf of bound Volumes (arcs), each opening into a vertical scroll of daily 3-panel pages, with the rival's red-bordered episodes interleaved; total episode count lives here, huge. (3) YOU — the character sheet: protagonist card with earned title, Voice Cast (mentor/rival narrators, locked ones shown as silhouettes with "?"), Arc selection (7–14 day themed programs), Cour Pass subscription, settings. End Credits is reached from a single CTA on Today after 6pm (and via the evening push), not a tab — rituals are scenes, not destinations. Why three tabs: the product has exactly three mental modes — live today, collect the past, build the character — and anything more dilutes the one-verb-per-screen rule.

key_screens.job: Get a half-asleep human to commit ONE tap instead of snoozing.
key_screens.layout: Pure black, subtle animated film grain. Top-left: small red '● COLD OPEN' live tag. Center: enormous tabular 'EP 48' in white ultra-condensed type filling 60% of screen width. Lower third: the narrator's spoken line rendered as a classic anime subtitle (white, 2px black stroke) syncing with the audio. Bottom 25%: one full-width vermillion slab button 'ANSWER THE CALL'. Below it in 40% gray, small: 'filler (snooze 5m) — Rival gains +1'. Volume keys and lock button do NOT dismiss.

key_screens.job: Verify one physical action with zero reading required.
key_screens.layout: Top: quest name in 2–3 words max ('SKY PHOTO') plus a single pictogram. The middle 70% IS the proof mechanism: live camera viewfinder, or a fullscreen motion meter that fills as you stand/move, or step counter ring. Bottom: one giant shutter/confirm control. On success: 1-frame white impact flash, 4px screen shake, heavy haptic, and a 3px ink panel border draws itself around the proof. No text paragraphs anywhere. Free tier cannot skip; premium can re-roll quest type.

key_screens.job: Deliver the dopamine payoff and frame today's mission — the daily screenshot moment.
key_screens.layout: Full-bleed generated background (sunrise palette + halftone texture). A black bar smash-cuts in at a 12° diagonal carrying 'EPISODE 48', then the mission title slams beneath it in huge condensed caps. Audio plays immediately; subtitles appear line-by-line in the lower third. Bottom edge: thin film-strip progress scrubber. Top-right: share icon exporting a 9:16 title card video with waveform — built for TikTok/Stories. After audio ends, a single button: 'BEGIN EPISODE'.

key_screens.job: Show the episode in progress and offer Lock In — nothing else.
key_screens.layout: The screen is laid out as an actual manga page: three ink-bordered panels stacked vertically with diagonal gutters. Panel 1: today's title card thumbnail + mission text. Panel 2: this morning's Wake Panel (the proof photo, screentone-processed, episode number + timestamp in the corner). Panel 3: empty, watermarked 'TO BE CONTINUED', fills at End Credits. Above the page: a thin VS strip (your EP count vs rival's, tug-of-war bar). Floating bottom-center: large 'LOCK IN' pill. After 6pm the pill becomes 'ROLL CREDITS'.

key_screens.job: Deliver 20 seconds of context-specific hype with zero friction.
key_screens.layout: Four huge full-width stacked cards: GYM / STUDY / WORK / RESET, each with its own ink accent color and a kanji-style monogram. One tap → instant fullscreen playback: bold captioned lines punch in over animated speedlines, narrator audio runs, no player chrome at all — hold anywhere to exit. On completion, a small eyecatch stamp animates onto today's manga page.

key_screens.job: Make the streak feel like a manga collection you own, not a guilt number.
key_screens.layout: Top: total count 'EP 212' rendered huge. Below: a horizontal shelf of Volume spines (each volume = one 7–14 day arc) with generated cover art; the current arc sits left with a progress fill climbing its spine. Tap a volume → vertical scroll of daily pages, each the 3-panel page from that day; filler days render gray-toned; the rival's episodes appear interleaved as red-bordered panels telling their parallel story. Long-press any page to export it.

key_screens.job: Close today and pre-commit tomorrow in under 60 seconds.
key_screens.layout: Dark warm screen. Phase 1: today's stats auto-scroll upward like ED credits (wake time, quest, lock-ins, episode rating prompt). Phase 2: three stacked oversized inputs — rate today (5 big stars), tomorrow's mission (single line with 3 smart suggestion chips), alarm time (big wheel, defaults to yesterday). One final full-width button: 'ROLL CREDITS' → the day's page freeze-frames with a halftone 'TO BE CONTINUED →' stamp, then the 10s Next Episode Preview teaser plays as the screen fades to black.

key_screens.job: House identity progression and monetization without polluting the daily loop.
key_screens.layout: Top: protagonist card — name, behavior-earned title ('The 6AM Heretic'), level, current arc badge. Middle: Voice Cast horizontal carousel — narrator characters as bold silhouettes with signature accent colors (gruff mentor, taunting rival, deadpan kuudere strategist, chaotic senpai); locked voices are black silhouettes with '?', staged like character reveals. Below: Arcs grid — themed 7–14 day programs ('Dawn Gauntlet', 'Exam Arc', 'Cut Season'). Bottom: the Cour Pass subscription presented as a season-pass card listing premium powers (personalized AI episodes, full voice cast, custom arcs, volume export). Settings gear top-right.

concepts.design_language: COLOR: Ink black #0E0E10 and warm manga-paper white #F6F2E9 as the base pair; one hero accent, Shonen Vermillion #FF4D2E, used ONLY for primary actions and episode numbers. Rival content uses a colder Crimson #C2182B; mentor/premium moments use muted Gold #D9A441. Morning title cards pull daily-generated sunrise palettes; night screens invert to black-base. Screentone halftone overlays at 8–12% opacity on all imagery and large surfaces. TYPE: Display = ultra-condensed grotesque (Anton-class) with a −6° skew, reserved for episode numbers and title cards; a rough ink-edge variant for single impact words (LOCK IN, FILLER). UI body = a clean humanist sans (Inter/General Sans), 17pt minimum. Spoken audio always gets classic anime subtitle treatment: white, 2px black stroke, lower third. Episode numbers always tabular and enormous. SHAPE: hard edges everywhere — 2–4px corner radii max, 2–3px ink-black panel borders, diagonal panel cuts at 12°, buttons as wide flat slabs. No soft cards, no glassmorphism. MOTION: hard cuts and smash-ins, never gentle ease-in-out fades for hero moments; 1-frame impact flashes (white = success, black = fail); speedline wipes 180–220ms for scene transitions; 4px screen shake + heavy haptic on quest success and title-card slam; idle surfaces carry faint film grain. ILLUSTRATION SYSTEM: zero chibi mascots and zero faux-anime character art that could read as bootleg IP — narrator characters exist as VOICE + bold silhouette + accent color + typography only. User imagery is their OWN proof photos run through a posterize + halftone 'manga-fy' filter so every panel is screentone-styled reality. Title-card backgrounds are generated painterly gradients, never faces. SOUND: a 0.5s 'episode sting' on title-card slam, ta-dum hit on TO BE CONTINUED, distinct rival leitmotif.
signature_moments: The Title Card Slam — every single morning generates 'EPISODE N: [your mission]' over a unique sunrise background, smash-cut with sting and haptic; one tap exports a 9:16 video with the episode audio waveform. This is the daily TikTok-able artifact no alarm app has.

TO BE CONTINUED — finishing End Credits freeze-frames your day's manga page with a halftone 'TO BE CONTINUED →' stamp and a 10s narrator-voiced Next Episode Preview teasing tomorrow. Ending the day becomes a cliffhanger you set for yourself.

The Rival's Cold Open — the alarm is sometimes the rival's scene, not yours: they reference what you said last night, what you did yesterday, whether you snoozed. Snooze today and tomorrow's cold open canonically remembers it. An adversary with continuity, not a notification.

The Filler Episode — snoozing past your quest window doesn't kill the streak; it makes today a gray-toned 'filler episode' in your volume. Funny, survivable shame: canon resumes tomorrow, but the gray page in your collection stings more than any broken-streak popup.

Volume Binding — every 7–14 day arc binds into a manga volume with a generated cover and goes on your Saga shelf; your real mornings (actual sky photos, actual made beds) become screentoned panels in a collection you can flip through and, on premium, export or print.
concepts.why_user_loves_it: Because by day 30 the number isn't a streak — it's an episode count, and 'EP 30' feels like a show that's found its footing, not a chain they're scared to break. The Saga shelf holds two bound volumes of their ACTUAL mornings — real skies, real made beds, manga-fied — a collection with personal weight that a Duolingo-style counter can never have. The rival has a month of shared history now: it remembers the Tuesday they snoozed and the week they went 7-for-7, so the relationship feels like rivalry, not nagging. Arcs rotated twice, swapping palettes, narrators, and quest flavors, so the loop never went stale. Filler episodes meant one bad morning never nuked their identity — the system is canonically forgiving, which is why they're still here. And every few days a title card was good enough to post, which means the app keeps paying them in content. They don't feel tracked; they feel SERIALIZED.
concepts.first_5_seconds: First launch is a cold open, not an onboarding form: black screen, sting, then a title card slams in — 'EPISODE 001: THE ONE WHERE YOU STOP SNOOZING' — and a narrator line appears as an anime subtitle: 'Every protagonist's story starts the same way. Asleep.' One vermillion button: BEGIN. In five seconds the user understands the entire premise without reading a paragraph: my life is being treated as a show, each morning is an episode, the alarm is the cold open, and this app is the studio. The same rule holds on every screen forever after: one enormous episode-style headline that states where you are, one giant accent-colored verb that states what to do — legible to a half-asleep brain at 6:30am from arm's length."

concepts.name: WakeSaga: Episode One
concepts.tagline: Your alarm is the opening scene. Wake up like a protagonist.
concepts.core_loop: NIGHT (22:00-23:30): A push reads "Schedule Episode 48." User opens the Next Episode screen: confirm alarm time, pick tomorrow's Wake Quest from 3 chips, see a cryptic AI-generated episode title teaser ("EP 48: The Rain Won't Wait"). Takes under 20 seconds. A Live Activity starts on the lock screen: "EP 48 airs in 7h 14m" — every doom-scroll glance now shows the countdown instead of guilt. ALARM (06:30): Full takeover screen. Audio is a 10-second personalized AI jolt (rival voice: "Kai is already up."). No dismiss exists. One giant button: BEGIN QUEST. QUEST (30-90s): Do the real-world thing — canonical quest is Sky Check: shoot the actual sky out your window through a live camera with a manga frame overlay; other quests are shake-verified standing, bed photo, object hunt, pushup counter. REWARD (the dopamine spike): The app mints today's Wake Card — your real sky photo cel-shaded into an anime panel, stamped with episode number, exact wake time, quest, streak day, and a rarity frame (earlier-than-goal, milestone days, and rare weather roll holo/foil variants). Card flips in with an impact-frame flash and haptic slam. The 60-120s Morning Episode auto-plays under the card (Gemini TTS): cold-open recap of yesterday, today's named mission, narrator persona of your choice (Mentor / Rival / System). Completing your card unlocks the Morning Panel — only now can you see your friends' cards from this morning (BeReal rule). Friends who snoozed appear as Cracked Cards. DAY: Lock In button on the home tab — tap GYM/STUDY/WORK/RESET for a 20-30s hype clip; home/lock widgets show today's card and streak flame. EVENING: Optional one-tap day log ("Did you complete the mission? Yes / Mostly / Filler episode"), feeds tomorrow's cold open, then schedule the next episode. SUNDAY: the week's 7 cards auto-bind into a manga Volume cover with an AI arc title — the weekly share moment.
concepts.information_architecture: Three tabs plus takeover surfaces — deliberately thin because the app's real estate is the alarm moment and the lock screen, not in-app browsing. TAB 1 — TODAY (default): state-aware home. Pre-wake at night it shows the Next Episode teaser and countdown; post-wake it shows today's Wake Card, today's mission line, and the Lock In button. TAB 2 — PARTY: your 3-6 person crew. Top: the Morning Panel (today's manga page of everyone's cards, blurred until you've woken). Bottom: your declared Rival head-to-head strip (who woke first, current rivalry score). TAB 3 — SAGA: your collection. Shelf of weekly Volumes, scrollable card archive, and a character sheet (wake consistency = Discipline, quests completed = Resolve, earliest wake = Limit Break). TAKEOVER SURFACES (not tabs): the Alarm screen, Quest camera, Card Reveal + Episode player, Next Episode scheduler (slides up from Today at night). SYSTEM SURFACES: lock-screen Live Activity countdown at night, Dynamic Island in the final hour, home-screen widget rotating your party's latest cards (Locket-style ambient presence). Settings live behind the avatar in the Today header. Why: a half-asleep brain only ever meets one full-screen surface with one action; tabs exist only for the awake, social, collecting brain.

key_screens.job: Get a half-asleep human to press one button — nothing else.
key_screens.layout: Full-bleed near-black screen, red-shifted to be retina-safe at 6:30am. Center: massive 'EP. 48' in condensed display type, slowly pulsing with the alarm audio waveform behind it. Below it, one line: tonight's quest name ('SKY CHECK'). Bottom third of the screen is ONE giant button: BEGIN QUEST — the entire zone is tappable, thumb-reachable from a lying-down grip. No snooze button. A tiny 'pause 5 min' text link sits top-corner in 11pt; using it pre-cracks today's card and says so. Clock and date in small type top-center.

key_screens.job: Capture real-world proof with zero decisions.
key_screens.layout: Live camera opens instantly, already framed in a manga panel border with halftone corners — the user sees their room/sky pre-anime-fied via a live cel-shade preview filter. One control: a huge shutter ring bottom-center. Top: quest instruction in one line ('Point at the sky. Shoot.'). For non-camera quests (stand up, pushups) this screen swaps to a single giant progress dial driven by motion sensors with the same one-element layout. No retake menus; one reshoot allowed via a small redo icon.

key_screens.job: Pay out the reward and start the story — the screenshot moment.
key_screens.layout: Black frame, one white impact-frame flash, then the Wake Card flips in center-screen at 60% height: cel-shaded sky photo as art, episode number and title in display type, wake time stamped huge (06:31), streak day badge, rarity frame slamming in last with haptics (holo shimmer reacts to gyroscope). Beneath the card, the Morning Episode player auto-starts: waveform, narrator avatar chip, 90s scrubber. Two buttons under it: 'Share' (pre-rendered 9:16 story image, one tap) and 'Enter the Day' (primary). Audio continues on lock screen if they leave.

key_screens.job: Show exactly where you are in today's episode — one glance, one next action.
key_screens.layout: Header: your avatar (cel-shaded, evolves with streak) + streak flame count. Hero zone: today's Wake Card at 70% width, slightly tilted, tappable to replay the episode. Under it: today's mission as a single bold line from the episode ('Today: finish the chapter you keep avoiding'). Primary CTA bar pinned above the tab bar: LOCK IN — tapping expands four chips (GYM / STUDY / WORK / RESET), second tap plays the clip full-screen with a timer overlay. At night this whole screen morphs: card is replaced by the Next Episode teaser and countdown with 'Schedule EP 49' as the pinned CTA.

key_screens.job: Make friends' mornings visible — but only after you've earned it.
key_screens.layout: The day's page composes automatically as a manga page: each friend's Wake Card is a panel, panel size weighted by wake time (earliest riser gets the splash panel), ink gutters between. Before you wake, the page is visible but every panel is screentone-blurred with 'Wake up to read today's page.' Snoozers' panels render as Cracked Cards — fractured glass overlay, no caption, no lecture. Below the page: the Rival strip — two avatars facing off, today's wake times, a running tally ('Kai leads 4-3 this week'). Long-press any panel to send one of five stock reactions (no comments, no chat — keep it zero-toxicity).

key_screens.job: Make 30 days feel like an owned body of work.
key_screens.layout: Top: horizontal shelf of Volume covers (one per completed week) with AI arc titles on spines — tapping opens a swipeable reader of that week's cards. Middle: full card grid, holo cards glinting, cracked cards kept (failure is canon, not deleted). Bottom: Character Sheet — three big stats in RPG framing (Discipline %, Resolve count, Limit Break time) plus current arc name. Premium upsell lives here naturally: locked card frames and narrator voices shown as grayed collection slots, not popups.

key_screens.job: Deliver instant hype in under 3 seconds from tap.
key_screens.layout: Triggered from Today's pinned CTA. Full-screen context color (red for GYM, blue for STUDY, etc.), narrator avatar center, 20-30s clip plays immediately with bold kinetic captions of key lines. A single optional control: a focus timer chip ('25:00') that, when tapped, keeps a Live Activity timer running after the clip ends. Swipe down to dismiss. Free tier: 1/day stock voice; premium: unlimited, all personas, context-aware scripts.

key_screens.job: Set tomorrow's alarm in under 20 seconds and end the night with anticipation, not settings.
key_screens.layout: Slides up over Today after 21:00. Top: tomorrow's teaser — 'EP 49' + cryptic AI title + your rival's scheduled time if they've already set theirs ('Kai airs at 6:15'). Middle: time picker defaulting to your usual, big and thumb-friendly. Below: three quest chips (yesterday's quest pre-selected). One button: SCHEDULE EPISODE. On confirm: a 'preview reel' haptic flourish, the Live Activity countdown starts, screen dims to a 'see you at first light' end card.

concepts.design_language: COLOR: Dark-first, always (the app lives at 23:00 and 06:30). Base ink #0B0B10, surface #15151C, card paper #F4EFE6 (cards are the only light objects — they glow against the dark UI like a screen in a dark room). One signal gradient, 'First Light': deep indigo #2B1E66 → ember #FF5C2A → gold #FFC53D, used ONLY for streaks, rarity, and the alarm pulse so it never dilutes. Rarity frames: ink (common), ember (rare), holo-foil shader (legendary). Night mode after 22:00 red-shifts everything and drops brightness. TYPE: Display = a heavy condensed grotesque (Archivo Black or Anton) set tight, uppercase, for EP numbers and card titles — manga-cover energy; UI = Space Grotesk for everything functional; numerals (wake times, countdowns) tabular and oversized. Tiny decorative katakana captions accompany section heads (purely aesthetic, always paired with English). SHAPE: Cards at 16px radius with 3-4px solid ink borders and halftone screentone textures in corners; panels and dividers use hand-inked 2px strokes, never hairlines; buttons are full-width slabs, minimum 64px tall (half-asleep thumbs). MOTION: Anime timing, not iOS easing — hard 100-150ms cuts with 1-frame white/black impact flashes on completion events, smear-frame transitions between tabs, speed lines on the card flip, gyroscope-driven holo parallax on rare cards. Nothing floats gently; things SNAP and SLAM, with matched haptics (heavy transient on card mint). ILLUSTRATION: One unified cel-shade pipeline — user avatars, narrator portraits, AND user photos all pass through the same posterize/cel filter (4-5 tone bands, ink outlines), so every piece of UGC is automatically on-brand and every screenshot of the app is recognizably WakeSaga. Avatars evolve visually at streak milestones (day 7 jacket, day 30 scar/aura). Crack overlay for failed days is a designed asset, not an error state.
signature_moments: SKY CHECK CARD MINT — your real morning sky, shot live, cel-shaded into an anime panel and stamped with episode number, wake time, and rarity. Every user generates a unique, beautiful, provably-real artifact every single morning that is literally formatted for an Instagram story. The proof IS the content; no competitor turns real wake-up evidence into anime art.

THE MORNING PANEL — your friends' wake cards auto-compose into one daily manga page, panel sizes weighted by who woke earliest, and the page stays screentone-blurred until YOU complete your quest. BeReal's reciprocity lock fused with a collaborative comic: the group's morning becomes a co-authored page worth screenshotting on big days (everyone up before 6 = full splash page).

RIVAL TRASH TALK IN YOUR EPISODE — you declare a real friend as your rival; the AI narrator weaves their actual wake time into your morning audio ('She was up at 6:02. You slept through her entire warm-up.'). Personalized AI audio that references real social data is unownable by alarm apps and un-copyable by social apps.

THE CRACKED CARD — snoozing doesn't trigger a guilt notification; it mints a visibly fractured card that your party sees on today's page. Failure is dramatized as canon, not lectured. People screenshot their own cracked cards ('bro look what the app did to me') — shame converted into shareable lore.

SUNDAY VOLUME BINDING — at week's end your 7 cards bind into a manga volume with an AI-generated arc title and cover ('Vol. 4: The Exam Week Gauntlet'). A guaranteed weekly share ritual and the sunk-value engine: by day 30 you own four volumes of your own saga.
concepts.why_user_loves_it: Day 30 works because every retention lever is reward-shaped, never nag-shaped. (1) Variable reward stays fresh: card rarity rolls, daily AI episode titles, and weather/holiday card variants mean the morning mint is still a small gacha pull a month in. (2) Sunk value compounds visibly: 30 cards, 4 bound volumes, an avatar that has visibly evolved — deleting the app means abandoning a body of work, not a streak number. (3) The story actually knows them: the day-30 cold open references day 1 ('A month ago you couldn't see 7am. Look at you now.') — Gemini TTS makes the narrator feel like it has watched the whole arc, which is the protagonist fantasy delivered, not promised. (4) Social gravity without toxicity: the party page unlock means friends are a reason to wake, the rival tally is a reason to wake EARLIER, and reactions-only (no comments) keeps it warm. (5) Failure is survivable lore: cracked cards and 'filler episodes' let users miss days without shame-spiraling into churn — the saga continues, which is exactly how anime treats setbacks. The user isn't maintaining a streak; they're mid-arc, and you don't drop a show mid-arc.
concepts.first_5_seconds: First open is a cold open, not onboarding. Black screen, one impact flash, then: 'Your life is a saga. Episode 1 airs tomorrow morning.' Beneath it, a live mock Wake Card tilts with the gyroscope — showing tomorrow's date, 'EP. 001', a placeholder dawn sky, and a rarity frame shimmer — so the user instantly sees the artifact they'll earn. One slab button: SET EPISODE 1. Within 5 seconds a new user understands the entire proposition: this is an alarm, waking up completes an episode, and completing an episode mints that card. Time and quest selection come AFTER the button press, in the same 20-second Next Episode flow they'll use every night — the first run IS the daily ritual, so nothing has to be learned twice.

final.one_line_pitch: An alarm clock that runs your life as a shonen anime: the alarm is the cold open, standing up mints the episode, and missing a morning is a canon chapter — never a dead streak.
final.winning_concept: behavior-science ("Cold Open") wins the spine. Three lenses tied at 7.3, but behavior-science is the only one whose anime layer changes MECHANICS, not paint: the time-aware Home state machine (judges called it "the single best intuitiveness decision in the whole document"), the Cliffhanger Teaser (a Zeigarnik open loop that makes users curious about their own alarm — category-creating), the Title Card Slam (the highest-rated organic-share engine across all 15 verdicts), and failure-as-canon Knockdown Chapters (the only genuinely new answer to the month-2 churn cliff). dead-simple-utility and apple-craft scored equal but are execution disciplines, not products — so we keep behavior-science's narrative engine and import their discipline wholesale.
grafted_ideas: [dead-simple-utility] Onboarding IS the core action: first screen is a giant time picker with one gold BEGIN — setting the alarm is the entire first run, voice and quest defaulted, no carousel, no quiz, no account wall. (Judge: 'best-in-class first-run.')
[dead-simple-utility] The 'one gold element per screen' contract: exactly one accent-colored primary action exists on any screen, ever — an enforceable rule, not a vibe.

[dead-simple-utility] The Narrator Remembers: every episode is generated against the user's actual behavior log (grace uses, early wakes, near-broken arcs) so day-30 content is structurally about THEM — the anti-staleness moat all three judges flagged as the most stealable retention mechanic.

[apple-craft] The Dawn Rail: Alarm → Quest → Title Card → Episode → Card is a forced linear modal pipeline with zero nav chrome — two brains, two modes; the 6:30am brain literally cannot get lost because no other screens exist.

[apple-craft] Truth-based card rarity: foils earned only by verifiable real-world conditions — woke before sunrise (Daybreaker), bad-weather wake (Storm Riser), beat-your-alarm (First Light) — honest variable reward that can't be gamed by copy.
[apple-craft] The Ember: lock-screen Live Activity + StandBy night clock with a dimming ember that flares 10 min before the alarm — the nightstand phone is in-universe before the app opens.

[anime-native] Episode count, not streak: the headline number (EP 212) is additive and unbreakable at the data-model level — pride, never loss-anxiety. Arcs and knockdowns layer narrative on top, but nothing ever resets to zero.
[anime-native] Snooze as a visible, priced narrative choice: 'FILLER (snooze 9 min)' is a real labeled button, not a hidden hold gesture — escape is honest, the cost is story.
[social-viral] Onboarding-as-ritual: the first-run alarm setup IS the nightly Next Episode flow, so nothing is ever learned twice.
[social-viral] The Live Activity reframe: every nighttime doom-scroll glance at the lock screen shows 'EP 48 airs in 7h 14m' — hijacking the exact behavior that causes the problem.
final.core_loop: NIGHT (optional, 30s, never required): At wind-down time, one push: "Episode 48 is being written." Home is already in Night state: alarm time huge with a toggle (always armed by default — no nightly arming ritual, missing the ritual NEVER silences the alarm), tomorrow's quest chip pre-selected from yesterday, and one optional prompt: "Tomorrow's mission?" (voice-first mic button or 3 suggestion chips; skippable in one tap — skipping costs episode specificity, never guilt). Closing the app fires the 1.5s TO BE CONTINUED stinger and the generated Cliffhanger Teaser line ("Tomorrow the rival finds out about the essay…"). Episode audio generates server-side overnight; mornings need zero network. ALARM (6:30): Critical alert fires into the Dawn Rail takeover. 8s personalized Cold Open in the narrator's voice using their name and real history ("Oi, Maya. Day 48. You bought ninety seconds yesterday — was it worth it?") before the tone escalates. One giant crimson button: BEGIN QUEST. Below it, smaller but VISIBLE: "FILLER (snooze 9 min)" — honest, labeled, costs a grey panel. Week 1 additionally shows a plain-language safety line ("Sick? Hold here to end the alarm"). QUEST (30–90s): One instruction in 64pt, max 5 words; the sensor IS the screen (viewfinder with halftone frame, giant step counter, shake ring). Verification has a designed failure ladder: 2 failed verifies auto-offers a simpler fallback quest (20 shakes standing); 3 fails ends the alarm and logs a Filler — the alarm NEVER traps. On verify: white flash, heavy haptic, hard cut — the cut IS the confirmation. PAYOFF (90s, skippable from second zero): TITLE CARD SLAM — "EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON," generated from their own night input, smash-cut at 12° with halftone burst. Morning Episode auto-plays with anime subtitles (works on mute); SKIP is dim but present from frame one, and a 20s Short Mode exists for late mornings. Then the Wake Card mints: quest photo screentone-filtered into a manga panel, episode number, wake time, truth-based foil if earned. SHARE (9:16 export with the slam baked in) or it auto-files to Saga. Then the app tells them to leave: "Go. The episode is live." DAY (pull only, zero daytime pushes ever): Home shows one giant LOCK IN button with a first-week sublabel ("20s of hype before gym/study/work"); tap → 4 context chips → instant 25s clip, hold-to-exit AND a visible X. EVENING: loop closes. FAILURE: a missed morning is a KNOCKDOWN — a red-ink canon chapter, never a reset. Tomorrow opens with a Comeback Quest one notch harder; landing it makes the knockdown a plot beat. Earned Recovery Days (1 per 7-day run, max 2) auto-absorb misses. A fully dead arc archives as a finished volume on the shelf and Arc N+1 begins immediately. Episode count only ever goes up.
final.information_architecture: Two-mode architecture for two brains. MODE 1 — DAWN RAIL (80% of usage): Alarm → Quest → Title Card → Episode → Card as a forced linear modal pipeline launched by the alarm, zero nav chrome, no back button, no tab bar — forward is the only direction. MODE 2 — THE APP, exactly 3 tabs: (1) TODAY (default) — a time-aware state machine rendering Morning (episode replay + mission + fresh card), Day (giant LOCK IN), Night (End Credits inline + teaser), or Post-Miss (Comeback framing). FIX for the judges' shape-shifting complaint — two persistent anchors survive every state: the alarm time + toggle is ALWAYS visible and tappable top-center (opens the Alarm Sheet at any hour), and LOCK IN is ALWAYS reachable (full button by day, compact pill in other states). The clock changes emphasis, never removes capabilities. (2) SAGA — the identity shelf: episode count huge at top, arcs as bound manga volume spines, the card binder grid, knockdown chapters in red ink, season finale dividers. (3) CAST — narrator roster (fighting-game select with instant 5s voice samples), arc packs, settings gear, and the Protagonist Pass paywall sheet (never a tab). Plain-English labels everywhere in week 1: the snooze says "snooze," CAST not "Vault," no "Cour Pass." Lock-screen Live Activity (episode countdown + ember), StandBy ember clock, and a home widget carry ambient surface area so the app stays small. No feed, no friends, no inbox — the rival is a character, not a leaderboard; sharing flows outward to TikTok/IG only.

key_screens.job: Teach the whole product and complete setup in under 15 seconds with one decision.
key_screens.layout: Black screen, film grain, low narrator audio: 'You've snoozed through 4,000 mornings. This isn't one of them.' Hard cut + haptic thud → title card slam: 'EPISODE 0: THE ONE WHO COULDN'T WAKE UP.' Then one screen: 'WHAT TIME DOES YOUR STORY START?' in 28pt over a giant wheel time picker, one gold BEGIN button. Voice (Mentor) and quest (Get Up) are defaulted; name capture and notification/critical-alert permission are folded into the same flow with one line of plain copy each. No carousel, no account wall. This screen IS the nightly scheduler they'll see forever — nothing is learned twice.

key_screens.job: Get a half-asleep human vertical with one tap — and never trap anyone.
key_screens.layout: Full-bleed OLED black, no status bar. Top: 'EP. 48' pulsing in 96pt condensed italic with the Cold Open subtitle line rendered anime-style (white, 2px stroke) syncing to audio. Bottom 45%: one full-width crimson BEGIN QUEST slab, thumb-reachable from a pillow. Below it, 40% opacity but clearly legible: 'FILLER (snooze 9 min)' — tapping plays one rival line, dims the ember, greys today's panel. Days 1–7 only: a third line, 'Sick or can't do this? Hold to end alarm' (3s hold, ends everything, logs a Recovery Day if banked). Screen brightness ramps 20%→100% over 20s. No other elements exist.

key_screens.job: Verify one physical action with zero reading and a designed failure ladder.
key_screens.layout: Top: instruction in 64pt heavy caps, max 5 words ('PHOTOGRAPH THE SKY') + one pictogram. The sensor IS the remaining 75%: full-screen viewfinder with halftone frame and a shutter occupying the bottom quarter, OR a 120pt live counter (14/20) inside a filling ink-stroke ring. After 2 failed verifications a fallback card slides up: 'CAN'T VERIFY — 20 SHAKES INSTEAD' (one tap swaps quest); a 3rd failure ends the alarm and logs Filler. On success: 1-frame white flash, 4px shake, heavy haptic, smash-cut. No dialogs, no retake menus, no dead ends.

key_screens.job: Deliver the daily dopamine spike and frame the mission — the screenshot moment.
key_screens.layout: Black, then the generated title slams in at 12° with halftone burst: 'EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON' over a daily generated sunrise-palette background, holds 2.5s, auto-saves a clean frame. Settles into the player: narrator flat-cel portrait with 2-frame idle loop, gold waveform, subtitles line-by-line in the lower third (current line white, past lines 35%) so it works on mute. Controls: 64pt play/pause, SKIP visible from second zero, 'Short' chip (20s cut) for rushed mornings. Final 10s: today's mission line types on and persists; auto-advances to the Card Mint. Audio survives backgrounding via Dynamic Island — they WILL open TikTok; plan for it.

key_screens.job: Mint today's proof artifact and invite (never demand) the share.
key_screens.layout: Card flips in center at 70% height with one foil-shine pass and a 'shing': the real quest photo screentone-filtered into a manga panel (the filter is load-bearing — it makes a grim 6:30am bedroom shareable, and on dark winter mornings the generated title-card art becomes the backdrop instead so no card is ever an ugly black rectangle), episode number stamped like a chapter mark, wake time huge, receipt-style mono footer (weather, arc day). Truth-based foils (First Light, Daybreaker, Storm Riser) shimmer with gyro. One gold SHARE (exports 9:16 with the 3s slam baked in as video) + 'Done' text button. Done → Today. Card files to Saga regardless.

key_screens.job: Show the ONE right action for this moment — with anchors that never move.
key_screens.layout: Persistent anchors in every state: top-center alarm time + toggle (tap → half-height Alarm Sheet: oversized wheel, quest chips with one-word sensor notes, voice chips, SAVE), top-left 'ARC II · EP 48' chip → Saga. DAY: massive circular crimson LOCK IN button (60% width, 4s breathing), mission line above, today's card peeking at bottom. NIGHT: End Credits inline — one stamp row (CLEARED / SURVIVED / KNOCKED DOWN), mic-first mission field with 3 chips, then the Cliffhanger Teaser types itself under 'NEXT EPISODE…'; LOCK IN compresses to a pill bottom-right. MORNING: replay tile + mission + minted card. POST-MISS: Comeback Quest framing, red ink. No feed, no stats grid.

key_screens.job: Deliver 25 seconds of context hype in two taps, one-handed, gloves on.
key_screens.layout: LOCK IN → third-height sheet, four oversized chips: GYM · STUDY · WORK · RESET, each with an ink-brush glyph. One tap plays immediately: full-bleed context color, narrator portrait, kinetic captions of key lines, live waveform. Exits: visible X top-right AND hold-anywhere (the invisible-affordance trap is fixed). Optional 'Start a block' chip spawns a Dynamic Island timer ('LOCKED IN — 47:12'). Free: 1/day, counter shown only after it's spent; premium: unlimited + all voices.

key_screens.job: Turn 30 mornings into identity evidence the user refuses to abandon.
key_screens.layout: Top: episode count enormous ('EP 212') — additive, unbreakable. Below: horizontal shelf of bound Volume spines (one per 14-day arc) with generated covers; current arc's spine fills as it progresses, finale slot gilded. Tap a volume → vertical scroll of daily pages: title, card thumbnail, wake time; knockdowns in red ink with torn edges (canon, visible, never hidden); Filler days grey-toned, no red, no shame copy. Every finale: an arc-summary card ('ARC I: THE ONE WHO COULDN'T WAKE UP — 14 EPISODES, 2 KNOCKDOWNS, 1 COMEBACK') plus a one-time 2-minute finale episode narrating their real stats. Segmented toggle: Timeline / Binder (card grid, foils glinting) / Rival (taunt log — opt-out lives here too).

key_screens.job: Sell 'who narrates your life,' demonstrated not described.
key_screens.layout: Fighting-game roster: Mentor (free, unlocked), Rival, Captain, Quiet Senior, monthly guest — full-color cards with a small lock, never greyed husks. Tapping any card instantly plays a 5s sample speaking the user's REAL name and episode count — the demo IS the pitch. Sheet rises on locked-tap or after Episode 3's title card (sell after the magic, never before): three plain lines (personalized daily episodes with callbacks · full cast + custom arcs · unlimited Lock Ins + foil odds), annual pre-selected with trial badge, monthly, restore. Loss-framed around the fully-personalized 3-day Pilot Arc they already heard. No timers, no slashed prices. Free tier forever keeps: the alarm, one quest type, Mentor voice, weekly (not daily) generated episodes, and the card mint — the narrator never fully stops remembering, so the core promise survives non-payment.

final.design_language: INK-AND-SIGNAL, DARK-FIRST — the app lives at 10:30pm and 6:30am, both in the dark. COLOR: base #0B0E14 (OLED-true ink navy), surfaces #161B26, paper-white text #F2EFE6 (warm, easier at 6am than pure white). ONE signal color per job, strictly rationed: Shonen Crimson #FF2E4C for the single primary action on any screen (alarm, quest, Lock In); Ember Gold #FFC93C reserved EXCLUSIVELY for rarity, foils, and milestones so gold keeps slot-machine meaning; knockdown red-ink #B3122E for canon failures; one semantic green #3DDC84 only for quest verification. No gradients except foil passes and the daily generated sunrise title-card backgrounds. Rule: one accent element per screen, ever. TYPE: two faces, hard limit. Display = ultra-condensed heavy grotesque (Anton-class), ALL-CAPS, −6° skew, used ONLY for episode numbers, title cards, and card text. UI = clean geometric sans (Inter), 17pt minimum body, 700 weight for actions, tabular oversized numerals for times. Spoken audio always gets classic anime subtitle treatment: white, 2px black stroke, lower third. SHAPE: manga, not bubbly — 4px radii on panels/cards with 2px ink borders and 12° diagonal slash dividers; native 20pt continuous corners on functional sheets and chrome. Panels never get blur; chrome never gets panels. Buttons are full-width slabs, 56pt minimum on any pre-9am screen. Halftone screentone at 8–10% on imagery and celebration surfaces only. MOTION: anime editing grammar — hard cuts, never crossfades, for hero moments; the SMASH CUT (6 frames of radial speed-lines, panel scales 1.15→1.0 with overshoot, 1-frame white flash, heavy haptic) and the CARD MINT (single confident snap + foil sweep + gyro parallax) are the only two signature transitions; the Lock In button breathes at 4s; loading states are film-leader countdowns; Reduce Motion swaps cuts for dissolves. HAPTICS: a composed score, not default taps — Power-Up (three rising transients + heavy thud) on quest verify, Mint (crisp snap), Rival (one low rumble on Filler), Ember (soft 0.5Hz pre-alarm). Flutter: platform channel to CoreHaptics with AHAP files; takeovers via SystemUiMode.immersive; slams are AnimatedScale+Opacity with a white Container flash — no Lottie for v1; screentone is one tiled PNG blend overlay. ILLUSTRATION: narrators as high-craft flat-cel 2D portraits with 2-frame idle loops (blink, breath) — faces, not silhouettes (anime-natives simp for characters; brutalist mystery boxes read as cosplay), but zero chibi mascots, zero clip-art, zero confetti, ever. User photos always pass the screentone filter. SOUND: foley on every confirm (taiko thud, blade shing for foils), distinct rival leitmotif; episode audio is the star, UI sound supports it. VOICE/COPY: 'taunt or salute, never nag' — second-person, short, references real history; rival tone has a hard guardrail: never taunts during knockdown recovery, and a Rival-intensity setting (off/light/full) lives in Cast.
signature_moments: THE TITLE CARD SLAM — 10 seconds after you stand up: hard cut, haptic thud, and a generated anime episode title for YOUR day ('EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON'), built from what you said last night, smash-cuts onto a daily sunrise background. The most screenshot-able second in the app, every single morning, exported as a 3s 9:16 video in one tap.

THE COLD OPEN WITH RECEIPTS — the alarm doesn't start with a tone; it starts with 8 seconds of your narrator saying your actual name, your episode count, and a callback to yesterday's real behavior ('You bought ninety seconds yesterday. Was it worth it?'). 'My alarm just called me out by name' is a self-recording TikTok.

THE CLIFFHANGER TEASER + TO BE CONTINUED — every night ends with a generated 'NEXT EPISODE…' line about tomorrow and a 1.5s black stinger as you close the app. You go to sleep mid-story; the only way to resolve the loop is to wake up. The app's best exit moment happens when you leave it.

THE KNOCKDOWN CHAPTER — miss a morning and nothing resets: the miss is written into your Saga as a red-ink canon chapter, and tomorrow opens with the comeback ('Every protagonist eats the floor in episode 12. What matters is episode 13.'). Failure becomes content; a dead arc archives as a finished volume, never a zero.

FIRST LIGHT FOILS — truth-based rarity: wake before your alarm fires and the card mints holographic with gyro shine; storm wakes and pre-sunrise wakes earn their own foils. The rarest behaviors produce the rarest collectibles, so users start racing their own alarm — the terminal form of the habit.

THE EMBER — from lights-out to dawn, a tiny ember lives in the Dynamic Island and StandBy clock, dimming through the night and flaring 10 minutes before the alarm, while the lock screen counts down 'EP 48 airs in 7h 14m.' Every doom-scroll glance now shows the story instead of guilt.
what_we_kill: The hidden 10-second hold-to-surrender (behavior-science) — every judge flagged it as a panic moment and 1-star generator. Escape is now visible, labeled, and honest: FILLER snooze always on screen, plain-language sick-day hold during week 1.
Unskippable / no-scrubber episode playback (behavior-science, anime-native) — forced 120s audio is hostile by month 2. SKIP exists from second zero plus a 20s Short Mode; skipping never punishes.

Verification dead-ends (all concepts were silent) — an undismissable alarm with a failing camera at 6:31am is the worst failure mode in the category. We ship the failure ladder: 2 fails → fallback quest, 3 fails → alarm ends, Filler logged. The alarm never traps.
Breakable streaks as the headline number (behavior-science's 'arcs end') — replaced by anime-native's additive episode count; arcs and knockdowns are narrative layers on a number that only goes up.

Mandatory nightly arming and homework (dead-simple's 'Lights out', anime-native's required End Credits) — the alarm is always armed by default; the night ritual is a 30s optional enhancer whose only cost is episode specificity.

The social party layer (social-viral) — Morning Panels, public Cracked Cards, and friend-dependent retention: brutal cold-start, public shame in disguise, and BeReal's proven decay curve. The rival is a CHARACTER with memory, not a leaderboard of friends. Sharing flows outward only.
Public failure shame (social-viral's Cracked Cards visible to the party) — knockdowns are private canon in YOUR saga; the rival never taunts during recovery, and rival intensity is user-adjustable.
Silhouette-only narrators (anime-native) — anime-natives want faces to attach to; we commission flat-cel character art instead of brutalist mystery boxes (and never bootleg existing IP).

Jargon-gated day 1 (all concepts) — 'Cour Pass', 'Vault', unlabeled 'LOCK IN', morphing buttons. Week 1 ships plain sublabels, the word 'snooze' appears, CAST not Vault, and the home screen's two anchors (alarm time, Lock In) never disappear across states.
Dashboard IA and 5-tab layouts (the prior over-structured attempts) — three tabs, two modes, one gold action per screen. If a screen needs explaining, it gets cut.
Timer-and-slashed-price paywall gimmicks — voice samples speaking your real name and episode count ARE the pitch; utility-app trust converts this demographic, desperation doesn't.
build_order: 1. ALARM ENGINE + DAWN RAIL SKELETON — reliable critical-alert alarm in Flutter (iOS first), full-screen takeover route stack (Alarm → Quest → Title → Episode → Card) with zero chrome, the visible FILLER snooze, and the week-1 sick-day escape. This is the trust floor; nothing else matters if the alarm flakes. Validate the degraded path (notification-tap → app open) since full lock-screen takeover has OS limits.

2. WAKE QUEST VERIFICATION + FAILURE LADDER — Get Up (motion), Sky Photo (camera + liveness), Shake fallback. Permission pre-flighting at onboarding (never mid-alarm), 2-fail fallback swap, 3-fail safe exit. Ship with three quests, not eight.

3. TITLE CARD SLAM + WAKE CARD MINT — generated episode titles (one LLM call), sunrise backgrounds, screentone filter pipeline, truth-based foil conditions, 9:16 share export. This is the share engine; it works even before audio exists.

4. MORNING EPISODE PIPELINE — overnight server-side generation (Gemini script + Flash TTS), behavior-log memory store (wake times, grace uses, missions, knockdowns) feeding callbacks, subtitle-synced player with Skip/Short Mode, zero-network morning playback. Build the anti-sameness system in from day one: rotating episode formats (standard/rival/callback/quiet), tone register variance, and a template-similarity check before audio renders.
5. TODAY STATE MACHINE + ALARM SHEET — four states with the two persistent anchors, End Credits inline night flow (stamps, mic-first mission, teaser line), TO BE CONTINUED stinger.

6. SAGA SHELF — episode count, volume spines, daily pages, knockdown/filler rendering, arc finale cards. Lightweight v1: timeline + binder grid; the 2-min finale episodes ride the existing TTS pipeline.
7. LOCK IN — chips, instant player, free/premium gating, Dynamic Island focus timer. Smallest feature; do not let it grow.

8. CAST + PROTAGONIST PASS — narrator roster with live personalized samples, RevenueCat integration, Pilot Arc trial flow, post-Episode-3 paywall trigger, free-tier weekly-episode degradation (never full memory shutoff).
9. AMBIENT SURFACES — lock-screen Live Activity countdown + ember, StandBy clock, home widget. Pure retention polish; ship after the loop proves itself.

10. HAPTIC/SOUND SCORE + KNOCKDOWN/COMEBACK SYSTEM TUNING — AHAP compositions, foley pass, recovery-day economy balancing from real cohort data, rival tone guardrails. Then onboard to TestFlight with the 16–26 anime-native cohort and tune episode writing against real day-14 fatigue before any paid acquisition.
